reviews.review_description: So I am a nobody scumbag YT streamer and I do love a bit of jank in my games infact my channel prides itself on it, stellar plans looked promising a blade and gun style rpg with mythological monsters sounded awesome, I saw others reviews of which they gave next to no answer bar “I got this for free off the dev but it’s bad… I dunno why it’s bad but it is and it’s trash” THATS the reviews I saw, I disagreed I saw a crap review told him “you cannot review without an explanation as to why it’s bad” so I hopped back in after a year or so as I’d received a response from my original review which i wrote “it had potential but seems left unattended” (I’m paraphrasing) which the dev responded and gave me info on why it seemed abandoned, I excepted that tried the game and found the same glaring issues, now I’m not keen on helping devs fix their own games as I’ve paid for the bloody thing and I’m not gaining anything from this if anything I’m out of bloody pocket due to my foolish lack of expediency in refunding it so now I’m stuck with it, I will put my score back up in the hopes that the dev is ACTUALLY doing what they say, but I’m not hopeful, this one is a gamble folks, it may end up being all I wished it to be but I don’t deal in what ifs, hopefully this review is taken in the good will I intend as I’m not one to crap on a project if it has good bones, I wish you well so I shall leave my issues here and if they get heard great!, if not then I’ve just wasted my time yet again on a small dev, good luck my friend., (clunky movement you already mentioned working on, weapons handling is also clunky and for grip unreliable, melee combat either works well or not at all, the mechs make no sense to use as bar armor doesn’t seem to have any other benefit and removes the ability to pick up other items for the character model, the rpg elements are not explained well, small enemies (spiders) pop out of existence the moment they are dispatched knocking me out of the immersion instantly, projectile range of weapons seem shorter than the enemies with arrows range (I’m not sure on that one),abilities poorly explained and I had to figure them out by accident, this is by no means the worst game I’ve played on standalone but it does require more work and I’m not the guy to help fix a game I bought to enjoy, I wish this dev well and I hope whatever issue they may have dissipate and this becomes something I can come back to in a couple of years and eat my own words but until then this is my review.

developer_response.body:
Sorry for disappointing everyone.

Every single sentence in that review is true. I don’t want to argue, make excuses, or defend myself. I’m sorry.

Since I’m already here responding to this comment, I want to say a few more things.

First of all, thank you to everyone who bought the game and supported it.
And honestly, if you’re reading this review right now, please don’t buy the game anymore. It’s not worth your money.

It’s not that I don’t want to keep updating it.
The truth is… I genuinely don’t know what kind of updates could make people truly love this game anymore.

This project started with just 2 worlds and 5 maps.
Then it slowly grew into something much bigger:
different routes for each world, expanding and reusing existing maps, adding brand new maps, alien creatures, dragon-like monsters, story content, piloting mechs, and so many other ideas and concepts…

You can probably tell that my ambition for this game was always massive.

And that review was right about one thing too:
people liked the concept and the potential, but the potential was wasted.

I liked it too.
I really, really liked what this game could have become.

But honestly, I think I already reached my limit as a solo developer.
For this game to truly become what it was supposed to be, it would need a real team behind it.

I’m not trying to shift blame.
Right now, I simply can’t do that.

This was my first game.
And yeah… maybe it ended up unfinished. Haha.

But who knows about the future?

Maybe it stays unfinished forever.
Or maybe one day, when I finally have the ability, experience, and people around me to make it happen…

I’ll rebuild this game from the ground up, and finally create the version I originally dreamed of.

reviews.review_description: The movement is clunky you can't hold things correctly it's not worth it been 6 months since someone mentioned the guns being off kilter and no progress

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your honest feedback — I really appreciate you taking the time to share your experience.I’m sorry to hear the movement and interactions felt clunky to you. As a solo developer, I’ve been testing and playing the game myself for over a year and a half, and I personally feel the hand interactions, movement, and gunplay are in a good place. That said, I completely understand that everyone’s experience can differ, and your feedback is valuable.Could you please tell me more specifically which parts felt off? For example, is it the grabbing animation, the grip feeling, gun aiming, or something else? The “off kilter” gun issue you mentioned has been on my radar — I’d like to take another look at it based on your description.I’m still actively working on the game and want to make it better. Your detailed input would help me a lot.Thanks again for buying and playing — I’m sorry it didn’t meet your expectations.

reviews.review_description:
Stellar Plans can't be explained in a few words because it's a mix of many things. It's an action game with RPG elements. You will get attributes that you can assign to your character, plus you will find terminals throughout maps that will increase even more abilities. You are part of a futuristic sci-fi world who's fighting against a more primitive fantasy world. 

Your team will assign you tasks that you have to do into the other world. You will have close combat weapons like sword and axes, and guns like pistols and assault rifles. 

The worlds that you get transported into are very different from each other, it's very nice exploring all of them and every time feels like a new adventure. 

The game was previously released on PCVR, but the the Meta Quest version will include 3 extra maps. I've played the PCVR version in the past and I was pleasantly surprise at how smooth the game runs on the stand-alone headset. 

You know, something to keep in mind is that the game is made by a single indie developer. It's not perfect, but think about it, the game is only $7 and you get a full 5 to 8 hours campaign. The developer mentioned to me that as long as people are buying the game, he'll keep updating it.

developer_response.body: Sorry for the late reply, and thank you for your support! This is my first time developing a game, and I’m glad you enjoyed the world and mechanics. I’ll keep working on polishing the gameplay to make it even better!

reviews.review_title: A One Man Labour Of Love
reviews.review_description:
I was completely expecting to play this for 5 minutes and refund it in disgust. That didn't happen. This evening I thought I'd treat myself: So, I bought a certain AAA stealth parkour assassination game in the spring sale, thinking it would be a safe bet, and also got this as a bit of a punt. I ended up keeping this and refunding the former. Stellar plans is a game that reminds me why I love indie projects. It's one man's work, one man's vision and I have to say that It's really fun. It's a shooter/rpg hybrid that also has melee combat,  missions and lots of lovely loot. For a £6 game, the graphics are effective, charming and have a great sense of scale. In some cases and places, they're actually fantastic. The music and effects are pretty good, though note that the speech of NPCs is currently AI generated, but only as a temporary measure. The combat is fun, holstering is effective, enemy A.I is not bad, pretty good in fact for fire arm equipped enemies though slightly less so in melee. There are no rag-doll effects in melee, but you can slice enemies in half, which is nice.  Stellar Plans, has plenty of level ups for your character, nice weapons, full body representation and a lot of content and potential play time. It also has a lot of heart and 
 lot of enthusiasm. Sure it has some rough edges, but then I see enough of those in most Quest games. This is home brew game  production taken to an epic level.  A budget friendly, labyrinthine  labour of love. The dev must have been insane to undertake something like this on their own and if they weren't they probably are by now, but I'm really glad that this was made. Add to this usable power armour and mechs AND 2 very different faction campaigns and there is literally no excuse to not buy this game.

developer_response.body: Wow, this means the world to me! I’m incredibly grateful for your support and for giving the game a real shot. It’s amazing to hear that it resonated with you. I’ll keep working to make it even better!

reviews.review_description:
Love the concept of this game… an action rpg in a semi open world. I have only 2 issues with the game so far, and they’re big:  

#1 The firearm angles are usually off kilter when holding with 2 hands.  Makes it so I can’t use a revolver with 2 hands. 

And #2, make headshots worth more damage… no matter what I do, it always takes half my rifle clip to kill an enemy… doesn’t matter if I hit them in the head 10 times or in the foot, they’ll die with the same amount bullets.  

If these 2 issues can be fixed, I’ll change my score to 5 immediately. Great job devs. This could be a big game in the meta store if it’s updated a bunch.


Also, it’d be real nice if an AutoSprint option was added. I hate clicking my joystick so much cuz it leads to stick drift after too much use.

UPDATE 3/9/25
Welp that was quick lol new update just fixed my 1st issue making headshots more valuable than going to the body. Now aiming isn’t pointless. 

I still hope the dev can fix the off kilter gun angles on some of the firearms. Some weapons force you to shoot in unnatural positions when holding with 2 hands. 
But I’m gonna raise it to 5 stars just cuz the dev seems really intent on making his game better. For $6 it’s worth supporting an indie devs. Need more games like this

developer_response.body: Sorry for the late reply, and thank you for your support! This is my first time developing a game, and I’m glad you enjoyed the world and mechanics. I’ll keep working on polishing the gameplay to make it even better!

reviews.review_description: I don't know what game the other people who commented have played, but the one I played is not a good game. It's not just that it's bad graphically, which it is, it's that it doesn't make any sense. I bought it to support a developer, but the description does not match the reality of the game at all. I'm sure developer will do his best to improve the game and so I don't ask for refund. Meanwhile is a 2'5/5 game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to update your rating and review. I truly appreciate your support and your honest feedback.

reviews.review_description:
Game is alright to start. Would love to see some rag doll effects to bodies. However it seems this developer is working hard on this title. It has a ton of awesome ideas so far. Movement is a little bumpy in smooth turn mode. Overall excited to see where this game will be in time as still a work in progress title. 
   Much respect to the developer on this one. Hope to see some updates soon.

developer_response.body: Appreciate your feedback! Ragdoll effects and smoother movement are great suggestions. I’m working hard on improving the game, and updates are definitely on the way. Thanks for believing in the project!

reviews.review_title: Hours of content and endless potential
reviews.review_description: I entered this game and was thrown off by the initial clunkiness. Phasing through walls, wonky animation and graphics, and ai voice acting. Then, I kept playing. After that, I was met with ambitious gameplay features, vast open play areas, varying loot, enemy types, environments, mission types, and two separate hub worlds with great vibes and soundtracks overall. it's something that hit me in a way I didn't expect, and I understood exactly what was being aimed for. An urban-medival open zone combo in VR with a fantastic aesthetic is something that VR and gaming in general has yet to receive, and I hope this doesn't get abandoned, because the foundation right now is fantastic. Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: Thanks for sticking with the game despite the rough edges! I really appreciate your feedback and understanding of what I’m aiming for. I’ll keep improving it, and I hope to deliver something even better in future updates!

reviews.review_description:
It’s just amazing the whole world set up, quests, scenarios, environment, even enemies have unique attacking movements and fun bosses. Honestly if this was polished up it would be an easy win. 

Better blocking and weapon play, fluid movement, general responsiveness 

I’m sure the developer knows everything that needs to be improved but polished off I’d pay good money for the game

developer_response.body: Sorry for the late reply, and thank you for your support! This is my first time developing a game, and I’m glad you enjoyed the world and mechanics. I’ll keep working on polishing the gameplay to make it even better!

developer_response.body: Sorry for the late reply, and thank you so much for your support! We're continuously working on improving the game, and it means a lot to hear such positive feedback. We truly appreciate your enthusiasm and hope you keep enjoying the game!

reviews.review_title: This game has a lot going on
reviews.review_description: Stellar plans is a RPG adventure and sci-fi game I enjoy exploring the world and levels of this game, the single developer has done a lot in a little time. I'm having fun killing and being killed and enjoy the story line. It's like having two games in one. Can't go wrong for the price of a cup of coffee 👍😎 check out my YouTube channel VR play 86 to see game play or go to stellar plans  YouTube channel 👋

developer_response.body: Sorry for the late reply, and thank you for your support! This is my first time developing a game, and I’m glad you enjoyed the world and mechanics. I’ll keep working on polishing the gameplay to make it even better!

reviews.review_description: I couldn't play this for very long. It's just too rough and unpolished. Maybe once it gets an overhaul.

developer_response.body: I had a really pleasant and friendly chat with my friend about this project. Unfortunately, he gave it a two-star rating because he bought the Quest version after watching the PCVR promo video, expecting a similar experience. The system automatically refunded him, and he couldn’t change the rating, so it’s staying as is. Still, his feedback is incredibly valuable—huge thanks to him for trying it out! I’m committed to improving this project, and I hope this review doesn’t discourage anyone from giving it a shot. For anyone considering a purchase, I’d recommend checking out the Meta Quest promo materials beforehand, as there’s a noticeable difference compared to the PC version. Thanks, everyone!

reviews.review_description: What a excellent game well done guys loving it 5 🌟 all the way . Keep doing what your do .

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Your support means a lot, and I’ll keep pushing forward to make the game even better!
